recommendations.title: Establish Basic Information Security Management
recommendations.description: You need to establish a basic framework for information security management. Start with documenting your information security policy and defining clear roles and responsibilities.

recommendations.title: Strengthen Organizational Controls
recommendations.description: Improve your organizational governance around information security. Focus on supplier relationships and development security practices.

recommendations.title: Enhance People Security Awareness
recommendations.description: Invest in security awareness training and clear disciplinary processes for security violations.

recommendations.title: Improve Physical Security Controls
recommendations.description: Implement physical security measures to protect your facilities and critical infrastructure.

recommendations.title: Deploy Technical Security Controls
recommendations.description: Implement essential technical controls including access management, encryption, and monitoring.
actions: Set up security monitoring and logging

recommendations.title: Create Comprehensive Information Security Program
recommendations.description: You need a holistic information security program. Consider implementing an integrated management system.

recommendations.recommendation: The organization should review and enhance its documentation of organizational context, interested parties, and ISMS scope to ensure completeness, consistency, and alignment with current business and security requirements, in line with ISO/IEC 27001.

The organization should strengthen its improvement processes by consistently tracking corrective actions and proactively identifying opportunities to enhance information security controls and ISMS effectiveness, in line with ISO/IEC 27001.

The organization should enhance its performance evaluation processes by improving the frequency, documentation, and management review of security metrics, audit findings, and incidents, in line with ISO/IEC 27001.

The organization should strengthen operational security by ensuring documented procedures are consistently followed in day-to-day activities and supported by appropriate evidence, as required by ISO/IEC 27001.

The organization should improve its security support mechanisms by formalizing role assignments, maintaining competency records, and strengthening security awareness activities to ensure consistent staff capability, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27001.

The organization should enhance its existing risk assessment and treatment processes by ensuring they are consistently applied, properly documented, and regularly reviewed, as required by ISO/IEC 27001.

The organization should strengthen leadership involvement in information security by improving policy communication, management oversight, and ongoing review of security performance, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27001.

The organization should clearly define, document, and assign information security roles and responsibilities to ensure accountability for security activities across the organization, in accordance with ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should establish, document, and formally approve an information security policy that defines management direction and security objectives, and ensure it is communicated and accessible to all employees, in line with ISO/IEC 27001 and ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should strengthen existing segregation of duties by reviewing role assignments and improving employee awareness of security-related responsibilities, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should review and regularly update its list of external authorities and ensure relevant personnel are aware of escalation procedures for security incidents, in line with ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should strengthen engagement with external information security groups to ensure timely access to relevant threat intelligence and best practices, in line with ISO/IEC 27002 guidance.

The organization should enhance project security practices by consistently performing and documenting information security risk assessments for projects before implementation, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should strengthen acceptable use practices by ensuring employees formally acknowledge the policy and that compliance is monitored and enforced, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should regularly review and update the existing asset inventory to ensure it remains accurate and reflects current information assets, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
Implement and document controls for A.5.11 to meet ISO/IEC 27001 requirements.
Strengthen and formalize your implementation of A.5.12 (document the process, assign ownership, and review effectiveness regularly).
Strengthen and formalize your implementation of A.5.13 (document the process, assign ownership, and review effectiveness regularly).
Implement and document controls for A.5.14 to meet ISO/IEC 27001 requirements.
Strengthen and formalize your implementation of A.5.16 (document the process, assign ownership, and review effectiveness regularly).
Strengthen and formalize your implementation of A.5.15 (document the process, assign ownership, and review effectiveness regularly).
Implement and document controls for A.5.17 to meet ISO/IEC 27001 requirements.
Strengthen and formalize your implementation of A.5.18 (document the process, assign ownership, and review effectiveness regularly).

The organization should review and enhance employment terms to ensure information security responsibilities are clearly communicated, consistently applied, and kept up to date, in line with ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should improve its awareness and training programs by ensuring they are delivered consistently, updated regularly, and aligned with current security risks, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should strengthen its disciplinary process by improving documentation, communication, and consistent application of disciplinary actions for security policy violations, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should enhance offboarding and role-change processes by ensuring access removal and asset return activities are consistently performed and properly recorded, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should strengthen confidentiality practices by regularly reviewing and updating non-disclosure agreements to reflect current legal, regulatory, and business requirements, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should improve remote working security by ensuring controls and guidance are consistently applied, communicated to employees, and reviewed as remote work practices evolve, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should strengthen incident reporting by improving employee awareness of reporting procedures and ensuring timely handling and follow-up of reported security events, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should improve physical entry controls by maintaining visitor records and ensuring visitors are supervised when accessing non-public areas.

The organization should review existing physical security measures and strengthen access controls and monitoring to ensure sensitive areas are consistently protected and access is limited to authorized personnel only.

The organization should strengthen existing physical protections by reviewing and improving locks, barriers, and monitoring systems to ensure consistent security coverage, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should enhance physical security monitoring by ensuring monitoring systems are operational, maintained, and regularly reviewed for effectiveness.

The organization should implement measures to protect facilities and IT equipment from physical and environmental threats, such as fire, flooding, power issues, and environmental conditions, in accordance with ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should restrict access to secure areas so that only authorized personnel can enter and work within them, in line with ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should introduce clear desk and clear screen practices to reduce the risk of sensitive information being viewed or accessed by unauthorized individuals when workstations are unattended.
The organization should enhance equipment protection by reviewing equipment placement and implementing additional safeguards where necessary, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should strengthen off-premises asset protection by improving employee awareness and ensuring security measures are applied consistently during remote or mobile work.
The organization should improve storage media management by ensuring lifecycle controls are consistently applied and documented, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should enhance the reliability of supporting utilities by reviewing existing protections and ensuring they are adequate to support business continuity, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should protect power and data cabling to prevent damage, interference, or unauthorized access, in line with ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should improve equipment maintenance practices by ensuring maintenance activities are planned, documented, and consistently executed, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should ensure that sensitive data is securely removed or destroyed before equipment is disposed of or reused, to prevent data leakage or unauthorized recovery.
The organization should strengthen privileged access management by improving monitoring, periodic access reviews, and enforcement of least privilege, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should improve access restriction practices by ensuring role-based access controls are consistently applied, reviewed, and updated as responsibilities change, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should strengthen source code access controls by improving approvals, monitoring, and periodic reviews of access rights, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should strengthen malware protection by ensuring controls are consistently deployed, updated, monitored, and supported by staff awareness, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should establish a process to identify, assess, and address technical vulnerabilities in a timely manner (e.g., via vulnerability scanning, assessment, and remediation), in line with ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should strengthen configuration management by improving documentation, change control, and regular review of baseline configurations, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should strengthen information deletion by ensuring deletion methods are consistent, verified where appropriate, and aligned with retention requirements, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should secure, manage, and control networks to protect information in systems and applications (e.g., firewalls, intrusion detection, and segmentation), in line with ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should ensure network services are secured and monitored to protect information and reduce the risk of unauthorized access or disruption, in accordance with ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should implement network segregation to separate users, systems, or services with different security needs to reduce risk, in line with ISO/IEC 27002.
Implement and document controls for SDLC_GATE to meet ISO/IEC 27001 requirements.

The organization should implement a secure development life cycle that considers information security throughout planning, design, development, testing, and deployment, in accordance with ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should define and implement application security requirements before developing or acquiring applications to ensure security needs are addressed, in line with ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should apply secure system architecture and engineering principles when building or modifying systems to reduce security risks, in accordance with ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should implement secure coding practices to reduce vulnerabilities in software and ensure security is built into code, in line with ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should ensure systems are tested for security issues before release into production, and ensure test approaches protect sensitive data, in accordance with ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should strengthen outsourced development security by improving contract requirements, vendor oversight, and regular reviews of supplier security performance, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
The organization should strengthen change management by improving consistency of approvals, testing evidence, documentation, and post-change review, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.

The organization should strengthen audit testing protections by improving control of test activities, documentation, approvals, and safeguarding of information during testing, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.

ABC Company should review and enhance its documentation of organizational context, interested parties, and ISMS scope to ensure completeness, consistency, and alignment with current business and security requirements, in line with ISO/IEC 27001.

ABC Company should establish a formal and repeatable information security risk assessment and risk treatment process, including documented decisions and mapped security controls, to systematically manage security risks in line with ISO/IEC 27001.

ABC Company should improve its security support mechanisms by formalizing role assignments, maintaining competency records, and strengthening security awareness activities to ensure consistent staff capability, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27001.

ABC Company should strengthen operational security by ensuring documented procedures are consistently followed in day-to-day activities and supported by appropriate evidence, as required by ISO/IEC 27001.

ABC Company should establish regular monitoring, internal audit, and management review activities to evaluate the effectiveness of information security controls and ISMS performance, as required by ISO/IEC 27001.

ABC Company should clearly define, document, and assign information security roles and responsibilities to ensure accountability for security activities across the organization, in accordance with ISO/IEC 27002.

ABC Company should establish, document, and formally approve an information security policy that defines management direction and security objectives, and ensure it is communicated and accessible to all employees, in line with ISO/IEC 27001 and ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should strengthen existing segregation of duties by reviewing role assignments and improving employee awareness of security-related responsibilities, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should review and regularly update its list of external authorities and ensure relevant personnel are aware of escalation procedures for security incidents, in line with ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should strengthen engagement with external information security groups to ensure timely access to relevant threat intelligence and best practices, in line with ISO/IEC 27002 guidance.

ABC Company should enhance project security practices by consistently performing and documenting information security risk assessments for projects before implementation, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should strengthen acceptable use practices by ensuring employees formally acknowledge the policy and that compliance is monitored and enforced, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should regularly review and update the existing asset inventory to ensure it remains accurate and reflects current information assets, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.

ABC Company should review and enhance its documentation of organizational context, interested parties, and ISMS scope to ensure completeness, consistency, and alignment with current business and security requirements, in line with ISO/IEC 27001.

ABC Company should establish a formal and repeatable information security risk assessment and risk treatment process, including documented decisions and mapped security controls, to systematically manage security risks in line with ISO/IEC 27001.

ABC Company should improve its security support mechanisms by formalizing role assignments, maintaining competency records, and strengthening security awareness activities to ensure consistent staff capability, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27001.

ABC Company should strengthen operational security by ensuring documented procedures are consistently followed in day-to-day activities and supported by appropriate evidence, as required by ISO/IEC 27001.

ABC Company should establish regular monitoring, internal audit, and management review activities to evaluate the effectiveness of information security controls and ISMS performance, as required by ISO/IEC 27001.

ABC Company should clearly define, document, and assign information security roles and responsibilities to ensure accountability for security activities across the organization, in accordance with ISO/IEC 27002.

ABC Company should establish, document, and formally approve an information security policy that defines management direction and security objectives, and ensure it is communicated and accessible to all employees, in line with ISO/IEC 27001 and ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should strengthen existing segregation of duties by reviewing role assignments and improving employee awareness of security-related responsibilities, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should review and regularly update its list of external authorities and ensure relevant personnel are aware of escalation procedures for security incidents, in line with ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should strengthen engagement with external information security groups to ensure timely access to relevant threat intelligence and best practices, in line with ISO/IEC 27002 guidance.

ABC Company should enhance project security practices by consistently performing and documenting information security risk assessments for projects before implementation, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should strengthen acceptable use practices by ensuring employees formally acknowledge the policy and that compliance is monitored and enforced, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should regularly review and update the existing asset inventory to ensure it remains accurate and reflects current information assets, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.

ABC Company should clearly define information security responsibilities within employment contracts so employees understand their obligations to protect organizational information and systems.
ABC Company should establish regular information security awareness training to ensure employees understand common security risks, policies, and their role in protecting organizational information.
ABC Company should strengthen its disciplinary process by ensuring it is clearly documented, communicated to employees, and applied consistently when security violations occur.
ABC Company should improve offboarding and role-change processes by ensuring access changes and asset returns are performed promptly and properly recorded.
ABC Company should strengthen confidentiality practices by periodically reviewing agreements to ensure they remain valid and reflect current business and legal requirements.
ABC Company should define security expectations and controls for remote working to reduce the risk of data loss or unauthorized access when employees work outside the office.
ABC Company should establish clear and accessible ways for employees to report suspected security incidents or events so issues can be identified and addressed quickly.
ABC Company should improve physical entry controls by maintaining visitor records and ensuring visitors are supervised when accessing non-public areas.

ABC Company should review existing physical security measures and strengthen access controls and monitoring to ensure sensitive areas are consistently protected and access is limited to authorized personnel only.
ABC Company should strengthen existing physical protections by reviewing and improving locks, barriers, and monitoring systems to ensure consistent security coverage, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should enhance physical security monitoring by ensuring monitoring systems are operational, maintained, and regularly reviewed for effectiveness.

ABC Company should implement measures to protect facilities and IT equipment from physical and environmental threats, such as fire, flooding, power issues, and environmental conditions, in accordance with ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should restrict access to secure areas so that only authorized personnel can enter and work within them, in line with ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should introduce clear desk and clear screen practices to reduce the risk of sensitive information being viewed or accessed by unauthorized individuals when workstations are unattended.
ABC Company should ensure IT equipment is securely located and protected against damage, theft, or unauthorized handling, in line with ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should define basic security expectations for protecting organizational assets when taken off-site, such as laptops or storage devices, to reduce the risk of loss or theft.
ABC Company should improve storage media management by ensuring lifecycle controls are consistently applied and documented, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should enhance the reliability of supporting utilities by reviewing existing protections and ensuring they are adequate to support business continuity, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should protect power and data cabling to prevent damage, interference, or unauthorized access, in line with ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should improve equipment maintenance practices by ensuring maintenance activities are planned, documented, and consistently executed, as recommended by ISO/IEC 27002.
ABC Company should ensure that sensitive data is securely removed or destroyed before equipment is disposed of or reused, to prevent data leakage or unauthorized recovery.
ABC Company should strengthen endpoint security by ensuring protective measures are consistently applied and regularly reviewed across all user devices.
ABC Company should improve capacity management by reviewing usage trends and adjusting resources proactively to avoid service disruptions.
ABC Company should manage and control system configurations to prevent insecure settings and unauthorized changes.
ABC Company should improve information deletion practices by ensuring deletion methods are applied consistently and verified.
ABC Company should implement basic network security measures to protect systems and information from unauthorized access.
ABC Company should improve oversight of outsourced development by strengthening agreements and monitoring vendor practices.
ABC Company should establish a structured process to review, approve, and test system changes before implementation.
ABC Company should conduct security audits in a controlled manner to avoid disrupting systems or introducing new risks.
ABC Company should implement data masking techniques to limit exposure of sensitive information when full details are not required.
ABC Company should improve data leakage prevention by reviewing existing controls and strengthening monitoring of high-risk channels.
ABC Company should improve backup practices by defining backup frequency based on business needs and verifying restore capability.
ABC Company should improve logging practices by protecting logs from tampering and ensuring they are reviewed as needed.
ABC Company should restrict and control utility programs that can bypass system controls to prevent misuse.
ABC Company should improve web filtering by reviewing filtering rules and monitoring usage trends.
ABC Company should strengthen cryptographic practices by reviewing where encryption is applied and ensuring it is used consistently.

ABC Company should create a repeatable risk assessment method: list key assets, identify threats and weaknesses, and rate impact and likelihood. Record results in a risk register.
ABC Company should document how each major risk will be handled (accept/mitigate/transfer/avoid) and list the exact controls or actions to implement, with an owner and deadline.
ABC Company should improve training by covering all staff consistently, refreshing it regularly, and checking understanding (short quiz or phishing simulation). Keep records.

ABC Company should improve competence management by completing the competency record for all relevant roles, keeping it up to date, addressing identified gaps with training, and periodically reviewing competence as systems and threats change.
ABC Company should improve consistency by collecting evidence every time, reviewing it regularly, and fixing repeated gaps (e.g., missing approvals, untested restores).
ABC Company should document and implement basic procedures for access control, backups/restore, incident handling, and change management (who does what, when, and how).
ABC Company should hold regular management reviews of security performance (incidents, audits, key metrics) and decide actions with owners and deadlines.
ABC Company should run internal reviews/audits at least annually, document findings, and track corrective actions until completed.

ABC Company should create a repeatable risk assessment method: list key assets, identify threats and weaknesses, and rate impact and likelihood. Record results in a risk register.
ABC Company should document how each major risk will be handled (accept/mitigate/transfer/avoid) and list the exact controls or actions to implement, with an owner and deadline.
ABC Company should improve training by covering all staff consistently, refreshing it regularly, and checking understanding (short quiz or phishing simulation). Keep records.

ABC Company should improve competence management by completing the competency record for all relevant roles, keeping it up to date, addressing identified gaps with training, and periodically reviewing competence as systems and threats change.
ABC Company should improve consistency by collecting evidence every time, reviewing it regularly, and fixing repeated gaps (e.g., missing approvals, untested restores).
ABC Company should document and implement basic procedures for access control, backups/restore, incident handling, and change management (who does what, when, and how).
ABC Company should hold regular management reviews of security performance (incidents, audits, key metrics) and decide actions with owners and deadlines.
ABC Company should run internal reviews/audits at least annually, document findings, and track corrective actions until completed.

ABC Company should complete its documentation of business context by capturing missing internal/external issues, clarifying impacts on information security, assigning ownership, and reviewing it regularly (and when things change).
ABC Company should complete the stakeholder list and fill missing expectations. Make sure the requirements are clear, owned by someone, and reviewed regularly (and when things change).
ABC Company should make the scope clearer and more complete (what’s in/out, key systems and data, third parties). Keep it controlled and updated when the business or IT changes.
ABC Company should create a repeatable risk assessment method: list key assets, identify threats and weaknesses, and rate impact and likelihood. Record results in a risk register.
ABC Company should document how each major risk will be handled (accept/mitigate/transfer/avoid) and list the exact controls or actions to implement, with an owner and deadline.
ABC Company should improve training by covering all staff consistently, refreshing it regularly, and checking understanding (short quiz or phishing simulation). Keep records.

ABC Company should improve competence management by completing the competency record for all relevant roles, keeping it up to date, addressing identified gaps with training, and periodically reviewing competence as systems and threats change.
ABC Company should improve consistency by collecting evidence every time, reviewing it regularly, and fixing repeated gaps (e.g., missing approvals, untested restores).
ABC Company should document and implement basic procedures for access control, backups/restore, incident handling, and change management (who does what, when, and how).
ABC Company should hold regular management reviews of security performance (incidents, audits, key metrics) and decide actions with owners and deadlines.
ABC Company should run internal reviews/audits at least annually, document findings, and track corrective actions until completed.
ABC Company should improve corrective actions by doing basic root-cause checks, prioritizing high-risk issues, and confirming the fix actually prevents the issue from happening again.

ABC Company should complete its documentation of business context by capturing missing internal/external issues, clarifying impacts on information security, assigning ownership, and reviewing it regularly (and when things change).
ABC Company should complete the stakeholder list and fill missing expectations. Make sure the requirements are clear, owned by someone, and reviewed regularly (and when things change).
ABC Company should make the scope clearer and more complete (what’s in/out, key systems and data, third parties). Keep it controlled and updated when the business or IT changes.
ABC Company should create a repeatable risk assessment method: list key assets, identify threats and weaknesses, and rate impact and likelihood. Record results in a risk register.
ABC Company should document how each major risk will be handled (accept/mitigate/transfer/avoid) and list the exact controls or actions to implement, with an owner and deadline.
ABC Company should improve training by covering all staff consistently, refreshing it regularly, and checking understanding (short quiz or phishing simulation). Keep records.

ABC Company should improve competence management by completing the competency record for all relevant roles, keeping it up to date, addressing identified gaps with training, and periodically reviewing competence as systems and threats change.
ABC Company should improve consistency by collecting evidence every time, reviewing it regularly, and fixing repeated gaps (e.g., missing approvals, untested restores).
ABC Company should document and implement basic procedures for access control, backups/restore, incident handling, and change management (who does what, when, and how).
ABC Company should hold regular management reviews of security performance (incidents, audits, key metrics) and decide actions with owners and deadlines.
ABC Company should run internal reviews/audits at least annually, document findings, and track corrective actions until completed.
ABC Company should improve corrective actions by doing basic root-cause checks, prioritizing high-risk issues, and confirming the fix actually prevents the issue from happening again.

ABC Company should define key information security responsibilities (governance, risk, access, incident handling, and supplier security) and assign named owners for each. Document responsibilities (for example a responsibility assignment matrix (RACI)) and communicate them to relevant staff, including how to request approvals, handle exceptions, and report incidents.
ABC Company should create a clear Information Security Policy, get management approval, publish it to all staff (including new joiners), and assign an owner and review date.

ABC Company should expand segregation of duties to all critical processes, formalize approval workflows, periodically review exceptions and conflicts, and make security role awareness consistent through onboarding and refresh training with tracked completion.
ABC Company should keep the authority contact list current (owner and review cycle) and ensure relevant staff know when and how to engage authorities as part of incident escalation procedures.
ABC Company should make participation effective by defining what is monitored, how updates are triaged, and how learnings feed into risk management and control improvements.

ABC Company should ensure security is applied to all projects consistently by defining clear security checkpoints in the project lifecycle, standardizing the risk assessment method, keeping records, and verifying required controls are implemented before go-live.

ABC Company should create an Acceptable Use Policy (AUP) covering devices, email, internet, data handling, prohibited actions, and reporting requirements, publish it to staff, and implement a formal acknowledgement process during onboarding and periodic refresh with records.

ABC Company should complete missing assets and owners, define minimum required fields, reconcile the inventory against reality periodically, and align updates with change management so the inventory stays accurate over time.

ABC Company should make asset return and access removal consistent across teams by standardizing the checklist, integrating human resources and information technology triggers, recording completion for every case, and periodically reviewing records to confirm nothing is missed.

ABC Company should define a simple information classification scheme (for example Public, Internal, Confidential, and Restricted), assign owners for key data sets, and document handling rules for storage, sharing, encryption, and retention. Train staff to apply the scheme consistently.

ABC Company should improve labelling by making it consistent across key tools (for example email, documents, and file storage), reinforcing correct use through awareness, and introducing technical enforcement where feasible for high-risk information sharing.

ABC Company should define approved methods for transferring information and implement protections to prevent data leakage, such as secure transfer tools, encryption, access controls, and approvals for high-risk transfers. Implement monitoring where appropriate to detect repeated leakage risks.

ABC Company should strengthen identity management by standardizing processes across all systems, improving timeliness of account updates, reconciling accounts regularly to detect orphaned identities, and automating provisioning and deprovisioning where feasible.

ABC Company should tighten high-risk access and ensure access control rules apply consistently across all key systems, keeping evidence of approvals and periodic reviews to prevent accumulation of unnecessary privileges.

ABC Company should define and enforce rules for authentication information (for example passwords, personal identification numbers (PINs), and multi-factor authentication (MFA)), including minimum standards, secure storage and handling (no sharing), secure reset procedures, and protection of authentication secrets.

ABC Company should improve access reviews by making them consistent across all key systems, using a repeatable schedule, tracking removals to completion, and reporting recurring access issues so they can be corrected.

ABC Company should define and document an incident management approach, including roles and responsibilities, reporting channels, triage and escalation steps, communication procedures, and a consistent way to record incidents and actions taken. Ensure staff know how to report incidents.

ABC Company should improve continuity readiness by ensuring mechanisms cover all critical services, keeping a documented Business Continuity Plan (BCP) current, and testing recovery regularly (including restore tests) with documented results and tracked improvement actions. The plan should include recovery objectives such as the Recovery Time Objective (the maximum acceptable time to restore a service) and the Recovery Point Objective (the maximum acceptable data loss measured in time).

ABC Company should strengthen compliance management by keeping the requirements register up to date, reviewing changes regularly, and ensuring obligations are implemented consistently with evidence across all relevant systems and suppliers.
ABC Company should extend record protection controls to all critical records, improve integrity and retention consistency, test recovery, and review retention and disposal practices regularly.

ABC Company should make compliance checks consistent by retaining evidence of outcomes, tracking corrective actions to completion, and addressing repeat issues through root cause improvements and targeted awareness.
ABC Company should improve independent reviews by ensuring adequate scope and frequency, keeping evidence, and verifying that corrective actions are effective after implementation.

ABC Company should document operating procedures for important systems and processes (for example access management, backups, change management, incident handling, and supplier onboarding), keep procedures accessible and version-controlled, and assign owners and review dates.

ABC Company should include clear information security responsibilities in employment and contractor agreements, including obligations to follow policies, protect confidential information, report incidents promptly, and comply with acceptable use and access rules.

ABC Company should implement an information security awareness and training program covering key topics (for example phishing, password hygiene, secure handling of information, and reporting of incidents), with onboarding training and periodic refreshers for all staff and contractors.

ABC Company should apply the disciplinary process consistently by documenting cases, retaining evidence of decisions, ensuring managers and human resources know how to use the process, and periodically reviewing outcomes for consistency.

ABC Company should strengthen offboarding and role-change controls by integrating human resources and information technology triggers, measuring timeliness (for example same-day deprovisioning for leavers), and periodically reviewing records to confirm the process is consistently followed.

ABC Company should ensure confidentiality terms remain effective by reviewing templates periodically, confirming agreements are signed before access is granted, and maintaining retrievable records for audits and legal needs.

ABC Company should define and implement remote working security controls, including approved devices, secure configuration, device encryption, strong authentication (including multi-factor authentication (MFA) where appropriate), secure remote access (for example a virtual private network (VPN)), and guidance for using home or public networks safely.

ABC Company should provide clear, accessible reporting channels for suspected security events and incidents (for example a dedicated email address, phone number, or ticketing route), define what to report and expected response times, and ensure staff know how to use the process.

ABC Company should improve entry controls by ensuring visitor logs are complete, retained, and reviewed when needed, and by applying supervision and badge controls consistently across all sites and teams.

ABC Company should strengthen physical access protection by standardizing how access is granted and removed, maintaining an up-to-date list of authorized personnel for secure areas, and periodically reviewing controls and access logs for effectiveness.

ABC Company should strengthen physical protections by addressing gaps in locks and barriers, ensuring secure areas are clearly defined and protected, and verifying that controls remain effective as layouts and operations change.

ABC Company should improve monitoring by ensuring coverage is appropriate, recordings and logs are retained securely, systems are maintained, and monitoring outputs are reviewed with clear ownership and escalation procedures.

ABC Company should protect facilities and information and communication technology equipment from physical and environmental threats (for example fire, flooding, overheating, and power instability) using appropriate safeguards such as fire detection, controlled temperature and humidity, and suitable placement of equipment.

ABC Company should restrict working in secure areas to authorized personnel only, define rules for supervision and visitor handling, and ensure access is granted based on need with documented approvals.

ABC Company should implement clear desk and clear screen practices, including locking screens when unattended, securing paper records, and preventing sensitive information from being left exposed in shared areas.

ABC Company should ensure information and communication technology equipment is securely located and protected against theft, tampering, and damage, including secure mounting where needed, restricted access to equipment rooms, and protection for portable devices.

ABC Company should define and enforce rules for protecting organizational assets when off-premises (for example laptops, mobile devices, and storage media), including secure transport, avoiding unattended storage, and requiring encryption for devices that store sensitive information.
ABC Company should improve media handling by applying procedures consistently, maintaining records for high-risk transfers and destruction, and periodically checking compliance with the process.

ABC Company should strengthen utility protection by ensuring safeguards are maintained and tested, monitoring is in place, and response procedures exist for utility-related incidents, with evidence of checks and corrective actions.

ABC Company should protect power and data cabling against damage, interference, and unauthorized access by using appropriate routing, physical protection, and access controls (for example secured conduits and locked risers where applicable).
ABC Company should improve maintenance by ensuring schedules cover all relevant equipment, records are complete, and any issues found during maintenance are tracked and resolved promptly.

ABC Company should ensure secure removal of data before equipment is reused, returned, or disposed of, using approved methods for secure erasure or physical destruction where required, and retaining evidence that sanitization was completed.

ABC Company should strengthen endpoint controls by ensuring consistent coverage across all devices, monitoring compliance (for example patch and encryption status), and remediating gaps promptly with documented ownership and evidence.

ABC Company should improve capacity management by defining thresholds and alerts, reviewing trends regularly, documenting decisions and actions, and validating that capacity planning covers critical services and dependencies.

ABC Company should strengthen vulnerability management by defining remediation targets, ensuring consistent scanning and tracking, prioritizing critical exposures, and verifying fixes (including re-scans or validation) with documented results.

ABC Company should implement configuration management by defining approved secure configuration baselines, controlling configuration changes, restricting who can change settings, and regularly reviewing configurations to prevent drift and insecure settings.

ABC Company should improve deletion controls by applying the same retention and deletion rules across systems, keeping evidence for sensitive deletions where required, and periodically reviewing that deletion processes are effective.

ABC Company should implement network security controls such as firewalls, secure configuration, access controls, and secure remote access (for example a virtual private network (VPN)) to protect systems and information from unauthorized access.

ABC Company should improve oversight by monitoring delivery against security requirements, reviewing evidence of secure development and testing, and tracking remediation of issues identified with suppliers to completion.

ABC Company should implement change management for systems and services, including risk assessment, review and approval, testing, rollback planning, and recording of changes with clear ownership and evidence of implementation.

ABC Company should perform technical assurance activities (for example vulnerability assessment and penetration testing) in a controlled manner with defined scope, approvals, and safeguards to avoid disrupting operations and introducing new risks.

ABC Company should implement data masking for sensitive information where full details are not required (for example in test environments, analytics, or support workflows) so exposure is reduced while still enabling legitimate use.

ABC Company should strengthen data leakage controls by tuning monitoring and data loss prevention (DLP) rules, covering all common channels (for example email, file sharing, and removable media), and reviewing incidents and near-misses to drive improvements.

ABC Company should strengthen backups by ensuring coverage is complete, performing regular restore tests with documented results, and addressing gaps such as missing systems, insufficient retention, or inadequate backup protection.

ABC Company should improve logging by protecting logs from tampering, ensuring accurate timestamps, defining retention, and ensuring logs are reviewed appropriately with clear ownership and escalation.

ABC Company should restrict and control the use of powerful utility programs and administrative tools that can bypass controls, including approvals for use, least privilege, logging of usage, and separation of duties where feasible.
ABC Company should improve web access controls by tuning filtering rules, monitoring trends and bypass attempts, and updating controls based on emerging threats and observed incidents.

ABC Company should strengthen encryption by ensuring consistent coverage across systems and data flows, improving key management practices, and periodically reviewing cryptographic configurations and certificates to prevent misconfiguration and expiry issues.

Strengthen and formalize your implementation of 10.1.Q1 (document the process, assign ownership, and review effectiveness regularly).

ABC Company should define key information security responsibilities (governance, risk, access, incident handling, and supplier security) and assign named owners for each. Document responsibilities (for example a responsibility assignment matrix (RACI)) and communicate them to relevant staff, including how to request approvals, handle exceptions, and report incidents.
ABC Company should create a clear Information Security Policy, get management approval, publish it to all staff (including new joiners), and assign an owner and review date.

ABC Company should expand segregation of duties to all critical processes, formalize approval workflows, periodically review exceptions and conflicts, and make security role awareness consistent through onboarding and refresh training with tracked completion.
ABC Company should keep the authority contact list current (owner and review cycle) and ensure relevant staff know when and how to engage authorities as part of incident escalation procedures.
ABC Company should make participation effective by defining what is monitored, how updates are triaged, and how learnings feed into risk management and control improvements.

ABC Company should ensure security is applied to all projects consistently by defining clear security checkpoints in the project lifecycle, standardizing the risk assessment method, keeping records, and verifying required controls are implemented before go-live.

ABC Company should create an Acceptable Use Policy (AUP) covering devices, email, internet, data handling, prohibited actions, and reporting requirements, publish it to staff, and implement a formal acknowledgement process during onboarding and periodic refresh with records.

ABC Company should complete missing assets and owners, define minimum required fields, reconcile the inventory against reality periodically, and align updates with change management so the inventory stays accurate over time.

ABC Company should make asset return and access removal consistent across teams by standardizing the checklist, integrating human resources and information technology triggers, recording completion for every case, and periodically reviewing records to confirm nothing is missed.

ABC Company should define a simple information classification scheme (for example Public, Internal, Confidential, and Restricted), assign owners for key data sets, and document handling rules for storage, sharing, encryption, and retention. Train staff to apply the scheme consistently.

ABC Company should improve labelling by making it consistent across key tools (for example email, documents, and file storage), reinforcing correct use through awareness, and introducing technical enforcement where feasible for high-risk information sharing.

ABC Company should define approved methods for transferring information and implement protections to prevent data leakage, such as secure transfer tools, encryption, access controls, and approvals for high-risk transfers. Implement monitoring where appropriate to detect repeated leakage risks.

ABC Company should strengthen identity management by standardizing processes across all systems, improving timeliness of account updates, reconciling accounts regularly to detect orphaned identities, and automating provisioning and deprovisioning where feasible.

ABC Company should tighten high-risk access and ensure access control rules apply consistently across all key systems, keeping evidence of approvals and periodic reviews to prevent accumulation of unnecessary privileges.

ABC Company should define and enforce rules for authentication information (for example passwords, personal identification numbers (PINs), and multi-factor authentication (MFA)), including minimum standards, secure storage and handling (no sharing), secure reset procedures, and protection of authentication secrets.

ABC Company should improve access reviews by making them consistent across all key systems, using a repeatable schedule, tracking removals to completion, and reporting recurring access issues so they can be corrected.

ABC Company should define and document an incident management approach, including roles and responsibilities, reporting channels, triage and escalation steps, communication procedures, and a consistent way to record incidents and actions taken. Ensure staff know how to report incidents.

ABC Company should improve continuity readiness by ensuring mechanisms cover all critical services, keeping a documented Business Continuity Plan (BCP) current, and testing recovery regularly (including restore tests) with documented results and tracked improvement actions. The plan should include recovery objectives such as the Recovery Time Objective (the maximum acceptable time to restore a service) and the Recovery Point Objective (the maximum acceptable data loss measured in time).

ABC Company should strengthen compliance management by keeping the requirements register up to date, reviewing changes regularly, and ensuring obligations are implemented consistently with evidence across all relevant systems and suppliers.
ABC Company should extend record protection controls to all critical records, improve integrity and retention consistency, test recovery, and review retention and disposal practices regularly.

ABC Company should make compliance checks consistent by retaining evidence of outcomes, tracking corrective actions to completion, and addressing repeat issues through root cause improvements and targeted awareness.
ABC Company should improve independent reviews by ensuring adequate scope and frequency, keeping evidence, and verifying that corrective actions are effective after implementation.

ABC Company should document operating procedures for important systems and processes (for example access management, backups, change management, incident handling, and supplier onboarding), keep procedures accessible and version-controlled, and assign owners and review dates.

ABC Company should include clear information security responsibilities in employment and contractor agreements, including obligations to follow policies, protect confidential information, report incidents promptly, and comply with acceptable use and access rules.

ABC Company should implement an information security awareness and training program covering key topics (for example phishing, password hygiene, secure handling of information, and reporting of incidents), with onboarding training and periodic refreshers for all staff and contractors.

ABC Company should apply the disciplinary process consistently by documenting cases, retaining evidence of decisions, ensuring managers and human resources know how to use the process, and periodically reviewing outcomes for consistency.

ABC Company should strengthen offboarding and role-change controls by integrating human resources and information technology triggers, measuring timeliness (for example same-day deprovisioning for leavers), and periodically reviewing records to confirm the process is consistently followed.

ABC Company should ensure confidentiality terms remain effective by reviewing templates periodically, confirming agreements are signed before access is granted, and maintaining retrievable records for audits and legal needs.

ABC Company should define and implement remote working security controls, including approved devices, secure configuration, device encryption, strong authentication (including multi-factor authentication (MFA) where appropriate), secure remote access (for example a virtual private network (VPN)), and guidance for using home or public networks safely.

ABC Company should provide clear, accessible reporting channels for suspected security events and incidents (for example a dedicated email address, phone number, or ticketing route), define what to report and expected response times, and ensure staff know how to use the process.

ABC Company should improve entry controls by ensuring visitor logs are complete, retained, and reviewed when needed, and by applying supervision and badge controls consistently across all sites and teams.

ABC Company should strengthen physical access protection by standardizing how access is granted and removed, maintaining an up-to-date list of authorized personnel for secure areas, and periodically reviewing controls and access logs for effectiveness.

ABC Company should strengthen physical protections by addressing gaps in locks and barriers, ensuring secure areas are clearly defined and protected, and verifying that controls remain effective as layouts and operations change.

ABC Company should improve monitoring by ensuring coverage is appropriate, recordings and logs are retained securely, systems are maintained, and monitoring outputs are reviewed with clear ownership and escalation procedures.

ABC Company should protect facilities and information and communication technology equipment from physical and environmental threats (for example fire, flooding, overheating, and power instability) using appropriate safeguards such as fire detection, controlled temperature and humidity, and suitable placement of equipment.

ABC Company should restrict working in secure areas to authorized personnel only, define rules for supervision and visitor handling, and ensure access is granted based on need with documented approvals.

ABC Company should implement clear desk and clear screen practices, including locking screens when unattended, securing paper records, and preventing sensitive information from being left exposed in shared areas.

ABC Company should ensure information and communication technology equipment is securely located and protected against theft, tampering, and damage, including secure mounting where needed, restricted access to equipment rooms, and protection for portable devices.

ABC Company should define and enforce rules for protecting organizational assets when off-premises (for example laptops, mobile devices, and storage media), including secure transport, avoiding unattended storage, and requiring encryption for devices that store sensitive information.
ABC Company should improve media handling by applying procedures consistently, maintaining records for high-risk transfers and destruction, and periodically checking compliance with the process.

ABC Company should strengthen utility protection by ensuring safeguards are maintained and tested, monitoring is in place, and response procedures exist for utility-related incidents, with evidence of checks and corrective actions.

ABC Company should protect power and data cabling against damage, interference, and unauthorized access by using appropriate routing, physical protection, and access controls (for example secured conduits and locked risers where applicable).
ABC Company should improve maintenance by ensuring schedules cover all relevant equipment, records are complete, and any issues found during maintenance are tracked and resolved promptly.

ABC Company should ensure secure removal of data before equipment is reused, returned, or disposed of, using approved methods for secure erasure or physical destruction where required, and retaining evidence that sanitization was completed.

ABC Company should strengthen endpoint controls by ensuring consistent coverage across all devices, monitoring compliance (for example patch and encryption status), and remediating gaps promptly with documented ownership and evidence.

ABC Company should improve capacity management by defining thresholds and alerts, reviewing trends regularly, documenting decisions and actions, and validating that capacity planning covers critical services and dependencies.

ABC Company should strengthen vulnerability management by defining remediation targets, ensuring consistent scanning and tracking, prioritizing critical exposures, and verifying fixes (including re-scans or validation) with documented results.

ABC Company should implement configuration management by defining approved secure configuration baselines, controlling configuration changes, restricting who can change settings, and regularly reviewing configurations to prevent drift and insecure settings.

ABC Company should improve deletion controls by applying the same retention and deletion rules across systems, keeping evidence for sensitive deletions where required, and periodically reviewing that deletion processes are effective.

ABC Company should implement network security controls such as firewalls, secure configuration, access controls, and secure remote access (for example a virtual private network (VPN)) to protect systems and information from unauthorized access.

ABC Company should improve oversight by monitoring delivery against security requirements, reviewing evidence of secure development and testing, and tracking remediation of issues identified with suppliers to completion.

ABC Company should implement change management for systems and services, including risk assessment, review and approval, testing, rollback planning, and recording of changes with clear ownership and evidence of implementation.

ABC Company should perform technical assurance activities (for example vulnerability assessment and penetration testing) in a controlled manner with defined scope, approvals, and safeguards to avoid disrupting operations and introducing new risks.

ABC Company should implement data masking for sensitive information where full details are not required (for example in test environments, analytics, or support workflows) so exposure is reduced while still enabling legitimate use.

ABC Company should strengthen data leakage controls by tuning monitoring and data loss prevention (DLP) rules, covering all common channels (for example email, file sharing, and removable media), and reviewing incidents and near-misses to drive improvements.

ABC Company should strengthen backups by ensuring coverage is complete, performing regular restore tests with documented results, and addressing gaps such as missing systems, insufficient retention, or inadequate backup protection.

ABC Company should improve logging by protecting logs from tampering, ensuring accurate timestamps, defining retention, and ensuring logs are reviewed appropriately with clear ownership and escalation.

ABC Company should restrict and control the use of powerful utility programs and administrative tools that can bypass controls, including approvals for use, least privilege, logging of usage, and separation of duties where feasible.
ABC Company should improve web access controls by tuning filtering rules, monitoring trends and bypass attempts, and updating controls based on emerging threats and observed incidents.

ABC Company should strengthen encryption by ensuring consistent coverage across systems and data flows, improving key management practices, and periodically reviewing cryptographic configurations and certificates to prevent misconfiguration and expiry issues.
Strengthen and formalize your implementation of 4.1.Q1 (document the process, assign ownership, and review effectiveness regularly).
Strengthen and formalize your implementation of 4.1.Q2 (document the process, assign ownership, and review effectiveness regularly).
Strengthen and formalize your implementation of 4.1.Q3 (document the process, assign ownership, and review effectiveness regularly).

ABC Company should implement change management for systems and services, including risk assessment, review and approval, testing, rollback planning, and recording of changes with clear ownership and evidence of implementation.

ABC Company should perform technical assurance activities (for example vulnerability assessment and penetration testing) in a controlled manner with defined scope, approvals, and safeguards to avoid disrupting operations and introducing new risks.

ABC Company should implement data masking for sensitive information where full details are not required (for example in test environments, analytics, or support workflows) so exposure is reduced while still enabling legitimate use.

ABC Company should strengthen data leakage controls by tuning monitoring and data loss prevention (DLP) rules, covering all common channels (for example email, file sharing, and removable media), and reviewing incidents and near-misses to drive improvements.

ABC Company should strengthen backups by ensuring coverage is complete, performing regular restore tests with documented results, and addressing gaps such as missing systems, insufficient retention, or inadequate backup protection.

ABC Company should improve logging by protecting logs from tampering, ensuring accurate timestamps, defining retention, and ensuring logs are reviewed appropriately with clear ownership and escalation.

ABC Company should restrict and control the use of powerful utility programs and administrative tools that can bypass controls, including approvals for use, least privilege, logging of usage, and separation of duties where feasible.
ABC Company should improve web access controls by tuning filtering rules, monitoring trends and bypass attempts, and updating controls based on emerging threats and observed incidents.

ABC Company should strengthen encryption by ensuring consistent coverage across systems and data flows, improving key management practices, and periodically reviewing cryptographic configurations and certificates to prevent misconfiguration and expiry issues.

ABC Company should complete its documentation of business context by capturing missing internal/external issues, clarifying impacts on information security, assigning ownership, and reviewing it regularly (and when things change).
ABC Company should complete the stakeholder list and fill missing expectations. Make sure the requirements are clear, owned by someone, and reviewed regularly (and when things change).
ABC Company should make the scope clearer and more complete (what’s in/out, key systems and data, third parties). Keep it controlled and updated when the business or IT changes.
ABC Company should create a repeatable risk assessment method: list key assets, identify threats and weaknesses, and rate impact and likelihood. Record results in a risk register.
ABC Company should document how each major risk will be handled (accept/mitigate/transfer/avoid) and list the exact controls or actions to implement, with an owner and deadline.
ABC Company should improve training by covering all staff consistently, refreshing it regularly, and checking understanding (short quiz or phishing simulation). Keep records.

ABC Company should improve competence management by completing the competency record for all relevant roles, keeping it up to date, addressing identified gaps with training, and periodically reviewing competence as systems and threats change.
ABC Company should improve consistency by collecting evidence every time, reviewing it regularly, and fixing repeated gaps (e.g., missing approvals, untested restores).
ABC Company should document and implement basic procedures for access control, backups/restore, incident handling, and change management (who does what, when, and how).
ABC Company should hold regular management reviews of security performance (incidents, audits, key metrics) and decide actions with owners and deadlines.
ABC Company should run internal reviews/audits at least annually, document findings, and track corrective actions until completed.
ABC Company should improve corrective actions by doing basic root-cause checks, prioritizing high-risk issues, and confirming the fix actually prevents the issue from happening again.

ABC Company should define key information security responsibilities (governance, risk, access, incident handling, and supplier security) and assign named owners for each. Document responsibilities (for example a responsibility assignment matrix (RACI)) and communicate them to relevant staff, including how to request approvals, handle exceptions, and report incidents.
ABC Company should create a clear Information Security Policy, get management approval, publish it to all staff (including new joiners), and assign an owner and review date.

ABC Company should expand segregation of duties to all critical processes, formalize approval workflows, periodically review exceptions and conflicts, and make security role awareness consistent through onboarding and refresh training with tracked completion.
ABC Company should keep the authority contact list current (owner and review cycle) and ensure relevant staff know when and how to engage authorities as part of incident escalation procedures.
ABC Company should make participation effective by defining what is monitored, how updates are triaged, and how learnings feed into risk management and control improvements.

ABC Company should ensure security is applied to all projects consistently by defining clear security checkpoints in the project lifecycle, standardizing the risk assessment method, keeping records, and verifying required controls are implemented before go-live.

ABC Company should create an Acceptable Use Policy (AUP) covering devices, email, internet, data handling, prohibited actions, and reporting requirements, publish it to staff, and implement a formal acknowledgement process during onboarding and periodic refresh with records.

ABC Company should complete missing assets and owners, define minimum required fields, reconcile the inventory against reality periodically, and align updates with change management so the inventory stays accurate over time.

ABC Company should make asset return and access removal consistent across teams by standardizing the checklist, integrating human resources and information technology triggers, recording completion for every case, and periodically reviewing records to confirm nothing is missed.

ABC Company should define a simple information classification scheme (for example Public, Internal, Confidential, and Restricted), assign owners for key data sets, and document handling rules for storage, sharing, encryption, and retention. Train staff to apply the scheme consistently.

ABC Company should improve labelling by making it consistent across key tools (for example email, documents, and file storage), reinforcing correct use through awareness, and introducing technical enforcement where feasible for high-risk information sharing.

ABC Company should define approved methods for transferring information and implement protections to prevent data leakage, such as secure transfer tools, encryption, access controls, and approvals for high-risk transfers. Implement monitoring where appropriate to detect repeated leakage risks.

ABC Company should strengthen identity management by standardizing processes across all systems, improving timeliness of account updates, reconciling accounts regularly to detect orphaned identities, and automating provisioning and deprovisioning where feasible.

ABC Company should tighten high-risk access and ensure access control rules apply consistently across all key systems, keeping evidence of approvals and periodic reviews to prevent accumulation of unnecessary privileges.

ABC Company should define and enforce rules for authentication information (for example passwords, personal identification numbers (PINs), and multi-factor authentication (MFA)), including minimum standards, secure storage and handling (no sharing), secure reset procedures, and protection of authentication secrets.

ABC Company should improve access reviews by making them consistent across all key systems, using a repeatable schedule, tracking removals to completion, and reporting recurring access issues so they can be corrected.

ABC Company should define and document an incident management approach, including roles and responsibilities, reporting channels, triage and escalation steps, communication procedures, and a consistent way to record incidents and actions taken. Ensure staff know how to report incidents.

ABC Company should improve continuity readiness by ensuring mechanisms cover all critical services, keeping a documented Business Continuity Plan (BCP) current, and testing recovery regularly (including restore tests) with documented results and tracked improvement actions. The plan should include recovery objectives such as the Recovery Time Objective (the maximum acceptable time to restore a service) and the Recovery Point Objective (the maximum acceptable data loss measured in time).

ABC Company should strengthen compliance management by keeping the requirements register up to date, reviewing changes regularly, and ensuring obligations are implemented consistently with evidence across all relevant systems and suppliers.
ABC Company should extend record protection controls to all critical records, improve integrity and retention consistency, test recovery, and review retention and disposal practices regularly.

ABC Company should make compliance checks consistent by retaining evidence of outcomes, tracking corrective actions to completion, and addressing repeat issues through root cause improvements and targeted awareness.
ABC Company should improve independent reviews by ensuring adequate scope and frequency, keeping evidence, and verifying that corrective actions are effective after implementation.

ABC Company should document operating procedures for important systems and processes (for example access management, backups, change management, incident handling, and supplier onboarding), keep procedures accessible and version-controlled, and assign owners and review dates.

ABC Company should include clear information security responsibilities in employment and contractor agreements, including obligations to follow policies, protect confidential information, report incidents promptly, and comply with acceptable use and access rules.

ABC Company should implement an information security awareness and training program covering key topics (for example phishing, password hygiene, secure handling of information, and reporting of incidents), with onboarding training and periodic refreshers for all staff and contractors.

ABC Company should apply the disciplinary process consistently by documenting cases, retaining evidence of decisions, ensuring managers and human resources know how to use the process, and periodically reviewing outcomes for consistency.

ABC Company should strengthen offboarding and role-change controls by integrating human resources and information technology triggers, measuring timeliness (for example same-day deprovisioning for leavers), and periodically reviewing records to confirm the process is consistently followed.

ABC Company should ensure confidentiality terms remain effective by reviewing templates periodically, confirming agreements are signed before access is granted, and maintaining retrievable records for audits and legal needs.

ABC Company should define and implement remote working security controls, including approved devices, secure configuration, device encryption, strong authentication (including multi-factor authentication (MFA) where appropriate), secure remote access (for example a virtual private network (VPN)), and guidance for using home or public networks safely.

ABC Company should provide clear, accessible reporting channels for suspected security events and incidents (for example a dedicated email address, phone number, or ticketing route), define what to report and expected response times, and ensure staff know how to use the process.

ABC Company should improve entry controls by ensuring visitor logs are complete, retained, and reviewed when needed, and by applying supervision and badge controls consistently across all sites and teams.

ABC Company should strengthen physical access protection by standardizing how access is granted and removed, maintaining an up-to-date list of authorized personnel for secure areas, and periodically reviewing controls and access logs for effectiveness.

ABC Company should strengthen physical protections by addressing gaps in locks and barriers, ensuring secure areas are clearly defined and protected, and verifying that controls remain effective as layouts and operations change.

ABC Company should improve monitoring by ensuring coverage is appropriate, recordings and logs are retained securely, systems are maintained, and monitoring outputs are reviewed with clear ownership and escalation procedures.

ABC Company should protect facilities and information and communication technology equipment from physical and environmental threats (for example fire, flooding, overheating, and power instability) using appropriate safeguards such as fire detection, controlled temperature and humidity, and suitable placement of equipment.

ABC Company should restrict working in secure areas to authorized personnel only, define rules for supervision and visitor handling, and ensure access is granted based on need with documented approvals.

ABC Company should implement clear desk and clear screen practices, including locking screens when unattended, securing paper records, and preventing sensitive information from being left exposed in shared areas.

ABC Company should ensure information and communication technology equipment is securely located and protected against theft, tampering, and damage, including secure mounting where needed, restricted access to equipment rooms, and protection for portable devices.

ABC Company should define and enforce rules for protecting organizational assets when off-premises (for example laptops, mobile devices, and storage media), including secure transport, avoiding unattended storage, and requiring encryption for devices that store sensitive information.
ABC Company should improve media handling by applying procedures consistently, maintaining records for high-risk transfers and destruction, and periodically checking compliance with the process.

ABC Company should strengthen utility protection by ensuring safeguards are maintained and tested, monitoring is in place, and response procedures exist for utility-related incidents, with evidence of checks and corrective actions.

ABC Company should protect power and data cabling against damage, interference, and unauthorized access by using appropriate routing, physical protection, and access controls (for example secured conduits and locked risers where applicable).
ABC Company should improve maintenance by ensuring schedules cover all relevant equipment, records are complete, and any issues found during maintenance are tracked and resolved promptly.

ABC Company should ensure secure removal of data before equipment is reused, returned, or disposed of, using approved methods for secure erasure or physical destruction where required, and retaining evidence that sanitization was completed.

ABC Company should strengthen endpoint controls by ensuring consistent coverage across all devices, monitoring compliance (for example patch and encryption status), and remediating gaps promptly with documented ownership and evidence.

ABC Company should improve capacity management by defining thresholds and alerts, reviewing trends regularly, documenting decisions and actions, and validating that capacity planning covers critical services and dependencies.

ABC Company should strengthen vulnerability management by defining remediation targets, ensuring consistent scanning and tracking, prioritizing critical exposures, and verifying fixes (including re-scans or validation) with documented results.

ABC Company should implement configuration management by defining approved secure configuration baselines, controlling configuration changes, restricting who can change settings, and regularly reviewing configurations to prevent drift and insecure settings.

ABC Company should improve deletion controls by applying the same retention and deletion rules across systems, keeping evidence for sensitive deletions where required, and periodically reviewing that deletion processes are effective.
